title: Phyllochlamys spinosa

intro: Phyllochlamys spinosa, also known as the spiny phebalium, is a small to medium-sized shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Phyllochlamys spinosa include flavonoids, coumarins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phyllochlamys spinosa is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
